summary: Split Kirby's copied Ness neutral-special code out of the old `ftKb_SpecialNNs.c`/renamed area into a new matching `ftkirbyspecialness.c`, renamed the remaining file to `ftkirbyspecialdonkey.c`, and moved the exported prototypes into a dedicated `ftkirbyspecialdonkey.h`. The build config now links `ftkirbyspecialness.c` as Matching while keeping `ftkirbyspecialdonkey.c` NonMatching. Item and motion-state users were updated to include the narrower Kirby-special header instead of the oversized `ftKb_Init.h`.
what_changed: `configure.py` replaced one NonMatching object, `melee/ft/chara/ftKirby/ftKb_SpecialNNs.c`, with two objects: Matching `ftkirbyspecialness.c` and NonMatching `ftkirbyspecialdonkey.c`.

`config/GALE01/splits.txt` split the former `ftKb_SpecialNNs.c` range: `ftkirbyspecialness.c` covers text `0x800FED38..0x800FF8EC` and sdata2 `0x804D9478..0x804D9484`, while `ftkirbyspecialdonkey.c` covers text `0x800FF8EC..0x80105BA8` and data `0x803CB720..0x803CB730`.

Added new `src/melee/ft/chara/ftKirby/ftkirbyspecialness.c` containing the Kirby-copied Ness neutral-special entrypoints and callbacks such as `ftKb_SpecialNNs_800FED38`, `ftKb_SpecialNNs_800FEDD0`, `ftKb_NsSpecialNStart_Anim`, `ftKb_NsSpecialNHold_Anim`, air variants, IASA, Phys, and Coll functions.

Renamed `src/melee/ft/chara/ftKirby/ftKb_SpecialNNs.c` to `src/melee/ft/chara/ftKirby/ftkirbyspecialdonkey.c` and removed the Ness-specific functions from that file, leaving the later Donkey/other copied-special code in the renamed object.
Added `src/melee/ft/chara/ftKirby/ftkirbyspecialdonkey.h` and moved a large block of function declarations out of `ftKb_Init.h`, reducing the initializer header by hundreds of declarations.
Moved the `ftKb_Init_803CB710` and `ftKb_Init_803CB720` scale data out of `ftKb_Init.c`; the diff shows matching-related symbol/split updates around these float objects.

Updated users in Kirby and item code to include `ftkirbyspecialdonkey.h` where they referenced copied-special callbacks, including `ftKb_SpecialNYs.c`, `ftKb_SpecialNZd.c`, `ftmotionstates.c`, Ice Climbers ice, Kirby Game & Watch chef pan, Kirby Yoshi special-N, Mewtwo shadow ball, Peach toad, and Sheik needle held.
`config/GALE01/symbols.txt` changed several formerly global float symbols in the affected sdata2 region to local `@` labels, matching the new object boundaries and data ownership.

`itmewtwoshadowball.c` gained an explicit `default: break;` in a switch inside `it_2725_Logic101_Destroyed`, likely as a small matching/control-flow cleanup in code that calls Kirby-Mewtwo copied-special logic.
smart_moves: Made the link step incremental: only the newly isolated Ness slice is marked Matching, while the renamed trailing `ftkirbyspecialdonkey.c` remains NonMatching.
Used split-file boundaries rather than trying to match the entire old large `ftKb_SpecialNNs.c` object at once, reducing the scope to the `0x800FED38..0x800FF8EC` Ness region.
Created a dedicated header for the copied-special declarations instead of continuing to expose many unrelated functions through `ftKb_Init.h`.
Adjusted `symbols.txt` locality for float data after the split, avoiding stale global symbols for constants that now belong to a smaller object.
Updated dependent item files to include the new targeted header, preserving cross-system references while decoupling item code from the huge Kirby init header.
Kept original address-bearing function names such as `ftKb_SpecialNNs_800FED38` where semantic names were still partial, which preserves searchable address anchors for later cleanup.
decomp_lessons: For large fighter copied-special objects, split by copied character or move region first, then mark only the proven sub-object Matching.
After object splits, update all three build/link surfaces together: `configure.py`, `config/GALE01/splits.txt`, and `config/GALE01/symbols.txt`.
Header bloat can hide dependency problems; moving declarations from `ftKb_Init.h` into a move-specific header made item and motion-state dependencies more explicit.
When data moves with an object split, revisit symbol scope and local labels, especially for small float/sdata2 constants that may have been incorrectly promoted to global names.
Preserve address comments in headers during large declaration moves; they make the refactor auditable and help future decomp work verify that prototypes stayed attached to the same text addresses.
Include cleanup is a legitimate part of matching work: once a code region is isolated, unrelated includes can be dropped from the renamed source and added only where actually needed.
naming_conventions: New source file names use the lowercase fighter-special style: `ftkirbyspecialness.c` and `ftkirbyspecialdonkey.c` rather than the older mixed-case `ftKb_SpecialNNs.c`.
Kirby copied-special functions retain the `ftKb_` prefix.
Ness copied-neutral-special callbacks use the `NsSpecial` stem in names such as `ftKb_NsSpecialNStart_Anim`, `ftKb_NsSpecialAirNHold_IASA`, and `ftKb_NsSpecialAirNEnd_Coll`.
Donkey copied-neutral-special callbacks use the `DkSpecial` stem in declarations such as `ftKb_DkSpecialNStart_Anim`, `ftKb_DkSpecialAirNFull_Phys`, and `ftKb_DkSpecialNLoop_Coll`.
Unknown or not-yet-semantic functions kept address-suffixed names such as `ftKb_SpecialNNs_800FED38`, `ftKb_SpecialNDk_800FF8EC`, and `fn_80100E0C`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Converted the former monolithic NonMatching `ftKb_SpecialNNs.c` object into a Matching Ness sub-object plus a remaining NonMatching Donkey sub-object.
Changed `splits.txt` text ranges so `ftkirbyspecialfox.c` ends at `0x800FED38`, `ftkirbyspecialness.c` starts at `0x800FED38`, and `ftkirbyspecialdonkey.c` starts at `0x800FF8EC`.

Kept stack-padding artifacts such as `PAD_STACK(20)`, `PAD_STACK(4 * 4)`, and small dummy arrays in the new `ftkirbyspecialness.c`, indicating the extracted C was tuned for matching rather than fully cleaned up.
Used explicit float literals and motion constants in the matched Ness file, e.g. `0.0f`, `1.0f`, `Ft_MF_None`, and `0x0C4C5080U`, preserving codegen-sensitive details.
Localized sdata2 constants at `0x804D9478`, `0x804D947C`, and `0x804D9480` to anonymous `@` symbols in `symbols.txt` after the split.
Retained helper inlines such as `ftKb_SetNsFlashAttr` and `ftKb_NsSpecialNStart_Anim_inline` inside the extracted file, which likely helped reproduce the original compiler structure.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or review bodies were present in the provided PR slice; no explicit reviewer feedback is available.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that mark `ftkirbyspecialdonkey.c` Matching.
Search for follow-up cleanup of `ftkirbyspecialdonkey.h` declarations moved out of `ftKb_Init.h`.
Search for later renames of address-suffixed functions `ftKb_SpecialNNs_800FED38` and `ftKb_SpecialNNs_800FEDD0`.
Inspect whether the local float labels around `0x804D9478..0x804D9480` were later given semantic names.
Find related PRs splitting other Kirby copied-special files into lowercase `ftkirbyspecial<character>.c` objects.
